verdict_reasoning: HSPA5 (GRP78/BiP) is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone retained by the KDEL motif. However, it is well-documented to translocate to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in cancer cells, stressed cells, and during immunogenic/apoptotic cell death — a phenomenon extensively studied as 'cell-surface GRP78'. On the tumor cell surface it acts as a co-receptor and is targeted by several antibody and ADC programs engaging the extracellular-facing pool. This represents a cell-state-induced (oncogenic transformation + stress) ecto-display with documented surface biotinylation and flow-cytometry evidence.
